title: nigella papillosa subsp. atlantica
intro: Nigella papillosa subsp. atlantica is a subspecies of the Nigella papillosa plant, also known as sea fennel or wild fennel, which is a type of flowering plant in the buttercup family. It is native to the western Mediterranean region and has been used for its medicinal and culinary properties for centuries. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Nigella papillosa subsp. atlantica can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or oil, and is also used in traditional cooking to add flavor and aroma.
